---
title: "Working with Distributions"
subtitle: "Elicit, fit, plot, and sample"
---

## Overview

Every concrete `monte` distribution exposes the same small API:

- `elicit(...)` constructs a distribution from expert-provided inputs,
- `fit(data, ...)` estimates distribution parameters from observations,
- `plot(...)` gives a quick visual check of the shape, and
- `sample(...)` generates Monte Carlo draws.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import monte as mt
```

## Elicit from expert judgement

Elicitation is useful when you have estimates rather than raw data. For example, an
expert might say that a typical delivery time is expected to fall between 8 and 14 days
with 80% confidence.

```{python}
elicited = mt.LogNormal.elicit(
    lower=8,
    upper=14,
    confidence=0.8,
    name="Delivery time, days",
)

elicited
```

The original elicitation inputs are stored alongside the fitted distribution parameters.

```{python}
elicited.params, elicited.elicitation_params
```

## Sample from a distribution

Use `.sample()` to draw values for a simulation. Pass a `seed` when you want reproducible
examples or tests.

```{python}
samples = elicited.sample(size=10_000, seed=42)
samples[:5]
```

```{python}
np.mean(samples), np.percentile(samples, [10, 50, 90])
```

## Plot a quicklook chart

Use `.plot()` to inspect the distribution before putting it into a model. The plot shows
the CDF with a lightly shaded PDF on a secondary axis.

```{python}
elicited.plot(color="steelblue")
plt.show()
```

## Fit from observed data

When observations are available, use `.fit()` instead. Here we create a small synthetic
dataset and fit a normal distribution to it.

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(7)
observed = rng.normal(loc=10.5, scale=1.8, size=100)

fitted = mt.Normal.fit(observed, name="Observed delivery time, days")
fitted
```

```{python}
fitted.params
```

You can use fitted distributions the same way as elicited distributions: plot them and
sample from them in a Monte Carlo model.

```{python}
fitted.plot(color="darkorange")
plt.show()
```

```{python}
fitted.sample(size=5, seed=123)
```
